PAGE.TITLE: Claude Code
PAGE.CONTENT:
Command line (dochttps://code.claude.com/docs/en/cli-reference
enHTML)
claude update Update to latest version.
claude --resume <id or name>
claude -r <id or name> Resume a specific session by ID or name.
claude -c
claude --continue Continue most recent conversation in current directory
claude --print "query"
claude -p "query" Print response without interactive mode.
claude --name <name>
claude -n <name> Set a display name for the session.
claude --dangerously-skip-permissions
claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions Skip permission prompts.
Commands (dochttps://code.claude.com/docs/en/commands
enHTML)
/login Sign in with Anthropic account.
/exit
/quit Exit the CLI.
/help Show help and available commands.
/usage
/stats
/cost Show session cost, plan usage limits, and activity stats.
/status Show Claude Code status including version, model, account, API connectivity, and tool statuses.
/model Set the AI model.
/effort Set effort level for model usage.
/compact Free up context by summarising the conversation so far.
/branch Create a branch of the current conversation at this point.
/clear Start a new session with empty context; previous session stays on disk (resumable with /resume).
/resume Resume a previous conversation
/plan Enable plan mode or view the current session plan.
/skills List available skills.
/context Visualise current context usage as a coloured grid
/memory Open a memory file in your editor
/cd Move this session to a new working directory.
/copy Copy Claude’s last response to clipboard (or /copy N for the Nth-latest).
/background Send this session to the background and free the terminal.
/run Launch and drive this project’s app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
/fork Spawn a background agent that inherits the full conversation.
/btw Ask a quick side question without interrupting the main conversation.
/color Set the prompt bar colour for this session.
/release-notes View release notes.
/feedback Submit feedback, report a bug, or share your conversation.
Commands (dochttps://code.claude.com/docs/en/commands
enHTML)
/dataviz Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualisation, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artefact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colours, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualisations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a colour formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file’s values for your brand’s. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualisation", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualise data", "categorical colours", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colours", "colour by series".
/loop [interval] [prompt] Run a prompt repeatedly while the session stays open.
Omit the interval and Claude self-paces between iterations.
Omit the prompt and Claude runs an autonomous maintenance check, or the prompt in .claude/loop.md if present.
